back_translation: For we know that if this tent which is our house on earth is dismantled, we still have a building from God, an eternal house in heaven, which was not built by human hands.
For in this tent we groan, longing earnestly to put on our dwelling-place which is from heaven.
For when we have put it on, we will not be found naked.
For we who are in this tent groan with heaviness, because we do not desire to be stripped, but desire to put on additional clothing, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
God who has prepared us for this very thing, He himself is the one who has given the Spirit as a deposit/down-payment to us.
Therefore, we are confident always, and we know that while we still reside in this body, we are still separated from the Lord.
For we live our lives by faith, not by seeing.
We are confident, and prefer rather to be separated from the body, and to go reside together with the Lord.
Therefore, we aim to be pleasing to him, whether we reside in the body or have separated from it.
For we all must appear before the judgment-throne of Christ, so that each person may receive what is suitable for the deeds done in his body, whether good or bad.
Therefore, since we know about reverent fear toward the Lord, we persuade people. We have already become clear before God, and I hope that we are also clear before the conscience of you.

We are not commending ourselves before you again, but we are giving you opportunity to take pride in us, so that you would have an answer for those who boast in things that appear outwardly, not in the heart.
For if we are out of our minds, it is for God; if we have a sound mind, it is for you.
For the love of Christ drives us, since we have judged thus, that one died for all, therefore all have died.
And he died for all, so that those who are still alive would no longer live for themselves, but live for him who died for them and was raised to life.

Therefore, from now on we will no longer know anyone according to the standard of the flesh. Even though we used to know Christ according to the standard of the flesh, now we will no longer know him in that way.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, that person is a new creation. Old things have passed away, behold, new things have come into being.
All these things come from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ, and gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
Namely, God reconciled the world to himself in Christ, not counting their trespasses against them, and entrusted to us the message of reconciliation.
Therefore, we are ambassadors for Christ, as if God were appealing/imploring through us. We plead for the sake of Christ: 'Be reconciled with God.'
God made him who did not know sin to become sin for us, so that we might become the righteousness of God in him.
